title: Tassadia ovalifolia
intro: Tassadia ovalifolia, also known as Mexican damiana, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and sexual dysfunction, due to its potential benefits in promoting relaxation and improving mood. The bioactive constituents of Tassadia ovalifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Tassadia ovalifolia is commonly used in herbal preparations such as tea, capsules, and tinctures, often blended with other herbs to enhance its effects.
taxonomy: Tassadia ovalifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This family is also known as the dogbane or oleander family. It is a relatively small plant species that grows in a variety of environments.
